_comment: Deterministic 'populated environment' scenario for test_dimsim_human_activity. Humans repeatedly perform a fixed pool of 10 predefined everyday tasks at seeded-random intervals, so every run is identical. Each location is anchored to a REAL furniture object in the DimSim `apt` scene by its scene name (`furniture`) — positions are resolved from the live scene at spawn, so the humans act at the actual bed/sofa/toilet/counter, which is what makes the recording useful for testing vision models and object detectors. The (x, y) coordinates are robot/map-frame fallbacks used only if the anchor is missing. `standoff` nudges the stand-point off the furniture so actors don't clip into meshes. Humans spawn as the real scanned-person model (model_url, converted from data/person/jeong_seun_34.obj — the same photogrammetry human the MuJoCo person-follow tests use); if the GLB fails to load the driver falls back to a simple colored cylinder.
